sanskrit:
किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा।
येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगाः सुखानि च॥
transliteration:
kiṁ no rājyena govinda kiṁ bhogair jīvitena vā
yeṣām arthe kāṅkṣitaṁ no rājyaṁ bhogāḥ sukhāni ca
literal_meaning: What use to us is kingdom, O Govinda; what use are enjoyments or even life itself—those for whose sake we desired kingdom, enjoyments, and happiness?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna experiences acute existential collapse: the very objects of desire (power, pleasure, survival) lose all value when the people who give them meaning are about to be destroyed.
psychological_state: Acute moral injury with anhedonia—complete loss of anticipated reward and future orientation.
trigger_scenario: A CEO on the eve of a hostile takeover realizes the deal will bankrupt lifelong friends and mentors; the stock, the bonus, the corner office suddenly feel worthless.
anchor_text: When those we love are gone, every prize turns to dust.
